transcription.text: Oh, hello. My name is Bill Buxton. I'm chief scientist of Alias Wavefront, the makers of Maya 3D software. Now, many of you would have heard of Maya. You may have heard of Maya, the software used by leading game developers and by virtually every company nominated for a major visual effects award since its inception. When I heard that our company was lowering the price permanently of our Maya Complete software from $7,500 to $1,999, I had a very particular reaction. One I'll attempt to recreate for you now.

segments.text: Oh, hello. My name is Bill Buxton. I'm chief scientist of Alias Wavefront, the makers of Maya 3D software.
Now, many of you would have heard of Maya.
You may have heard of Maya, the software used by leading game developers and by virtually every company nominated for a major visual effects award since its inception.
When I heard that our company was lowering the price permanently of our Maya Complete software from $7,500 to $1,999, I had a very particular reaction.
One I'll attempt to recreate for you now.
